pitch_rationale: Area-weighted across 9 roof segments from Google Solar API (imageryQuality=HIGH): 36.01° → 9:12. Solar's underlying DSM model reports ~5° MAE in published benchmarks (Satellite Sunroof, NeurIPS 2024).
footprint_rationale: Subject is a single townhome unit enclosed by the orange dashed bounding box (~192 × 250 px). At 0.1879 ft/px this yields a bounding box of ~36.1 ft × 47.0 ft = 1,697 sq ft. A hip-roof fill factor of ~83% gives a plan-view footprint of ~1,410 sq ft. The roof is a classic four-plane hip with no gable ends or valleys; all four sides terminate in eaves. Ridge runs east–west estimated at ~19 ft; four hip lines totaling ~88 ft; perimeter eaves ~166 ft. Confidence is moderate (0.57) due to partial tree canopy overlap at the south end of the subject roof and uncertainty about any small front-porch overhang.

condition.rationale: The subject roof within the orange bounding box is a hip-style asphalt shingle roof with multiple slopes converging to a central peak. The shingle surface displays a uniform medium gray/charcoal color tone across all visible slopes with no notable color breaks, patches, or discoloration. Ridge lines appear geometrically straight with no visible sagging or distortion when compared to the adjacent identical units in the row. No tarps, coverings, or foreign debris are visible on any slope. No green or dark biological growth patches are detectable on the north-facing or other slopes. No dark gaps or exposed decking areas indicative of missing material in bulk are present. Image resolution and lighting conditions are sufficient to assess the overall surface — no significant tree shadow or obstruction covers the subject roof. Overall, the visible roof surface shows no conditions warranting urgent closer inspection.

fence_reason: vision roof area 1763 differs from solar 2081 by 15.3% (>12%)
